observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGSO together with the Inventory Committee, submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) continue reconciling their records and make the necessary adjustments or corrections in the books and property records to ensure accuracy and reliability in the PPE account.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant establish and maintain complete and detailed PPE Ledger Cards, and that the LGSO-Designate do the same for Property Cards covering all LGU properties. This will strengthen record-keeping, ensure accountability, and support the accuracy of financial records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant drop the aforementioned properties totaling ₱11,894,270.08 from the PPE account to arrive at the correct account balance.
We also recommended that, moving forward, the Municipal Accountant comply with COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 and COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024 for the proper treatment of semi-expendable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, GSO and Municipal Engineer reconcile their Road Network records.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the correct and complete description and cost segregation of road components of all road projects.
Moreover, we recommended that the  Municipal Assessor inspect  and assess the cost of the road lot component of all road networks within the territorial jurisdiction of LGU-Inopacan and  provide data to the Municipal Accountant, GSO and Municipal Engineer.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the Road Lot component of the Municipal Road projects based on the report of the Municipal Assessor, maintain the LRNLC with complete description of each road component, and Disclose in the Notes to FS in the ensuing years the breakdown of the Road Network Account.
Likewise, we recommended that the Inventory Committee, use the required format provided in COA Circular 2015-008 in reporting the Physical Count of Road Network System.
Finally, we recommended that the GSO prepare and maintain complete and detailed LRNPC for all roads and its components and in the ensuing years, prepare the Report on Local Road Network.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary correcting entries for the above noted misstated accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide a written justification addressing the alleged illegality of the ordinance related to the unreleased loan from Land Bank. Additionally, the Mayor should submit to the Audit Team all supporting documents and records of communication with Land Bank regarding the loan status.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the Construction and Heavy Equipment and the corresponding Accounts Payable of ₱10,280,000.00 in the books of accounts. Furthermore, this obligation should be disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements, indicating that payment remains pending due to the unreleased loan proceeds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate subsidiary ledgers per fund transfer for the noted lumpsum amounts of ₱10,368,777.40 and ₱14,058,312.71 currently labeled as SL-NGA and SL-Previous years.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace back previous records and prepare necessary adjusting entries to arrive at a correct balance for the Due to NGAs account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in the preparation of succeeding Financial Statements, shall classify the current portion of Loans Payable - Domestic as Current Liabilities and shall provide detailed information as to the composition of the loan account in the Notes to the Financial Statement.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
make necessary adjusting entries for the noted double payment and the erroneous general journal entry; and
trace the root cause of the unreconciled variance in the Loans Payable – Domestic account balance and make the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entry/ies to restore the unreleased checks, stale checks and cancelled checks to the Cash in bank account to reflect the actual Cash balance of the LGU at the end of the year. Said entries shall be reversed when checks are released to the payees in the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer:
transfer the unutilized DRRM Funds of CY 2024 amounting to ₱2,841,016.95 to the Special Trust Fund account of the LGU; and
avoid using the General Fund for payment of PPAs charged to the Trust Fund and vice versa.
We also recommended that the Municipality continue to strictly adhere to the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the transfer of the unutilized QRF and DRRM-MOOE from the General Fund to Special Trust Fund, to avoid possible misuse of DRRM Funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the MDRRM Council, and the MDRRM Officer:
ensure full implementation of PPAs identified in the LDRRMFIP in order to effectively increase the level of preparedness of the municipality during natural calamities; and
Re-appropriate the balances of completed and unnecessary projects to other PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal DRMM Officer:
Stop the distribution of relief goods to non-calamity-stricken residents and strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 10121 to avoid committing prohibited acts in the implementation, distribution, and utilization of resources out of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund; and
Include as one of the conditions in the procurement of relief goods, the expiration dates at least six months from the delivery date to avoid spoilage or wastage of government fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, identify and include in the AIP priority projects which are ready for implementation in the current budget year to ensure optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local Development Council, the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Budget Officer submit a justification on:
the discrepancies between the PPAs identified in the budgets and AIP and the actual implemented PPAs as to name/title;
the Supplemental Budget No. 1 which exceeded the actual appropriations reverted; and
the lots purchased which remained untitled.
We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to issue check returning the second payment for December 2024 loan amortization from the DBP Maasin Account to the General Main Account and make the necessary journal entry for the return, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all insurable LGU properties are covered under the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), in compliance with R.A. No. 656 and the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2018-002.
In the future application of insurance with the GSIS GIF, we also recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer Designate prepare the PIF and submit the same to GSIS not later than April 30 of each year to ensure sufficiency of insurance coverage and protection in case of unforeseen natural or man-made hazards. Furnish copy of the PIF and proof of submission to GSIS to the COA Audit Team for evaluation of the actions taken.

We recommended that the GFPS Technical Working Group and GAD Focal Person provide the lacking breakdown of expenditures pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, as amended.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, with the help of the MSWDO/GAD Focal person, systematically gather updated data regarding various GAD information on gender statistics and age and sex- disaggregated data and consequently, update the existing Gender and Development database.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order to establish the GAD M&E Team, in accordance with the personnel requirements outlined in Section 5.2 of JMC 2013-01, and clearly define their roles and responsibilities.

We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO):
Reconcile records with GSIS and promptly update employees on any necessary changes to their employment status. These updates should be communicated to the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) using the prescribed GSIS format to ensure timely and accurate record updates; and
Ensure that all premium contributions and loan amortizations are deducted from employees' salaries in the payroll for the month they are due.
We also recommended that the AAO:
Promptly update employees' employment status and salary adjustments in the GSIS Web MPS upon receiving the necessary information from the HRMO; and
Monitor the approval of employees' loan applications and immediately inform the HRMO and Municipal Accountant to ensure timely deductions from payroll.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
provide the Audit Team with an explanation of the discrepancies identified in the GSIS billing, the amounts withheld from contributions and loan deductions, and the corresponding remittances as reflected in Tables 1 and 2; and
conduct monthly reconciliations of employee premium contributions and loan amortizations by comparing GSIS billings with actual payroll deductions and remitted amounts. This practice ensures billing accuracy, facilitates timely remittances to GSIS, and guarantees correct salary deductions

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the accounting records to verify the accuracy of the unaccounted/unsettled "Due to PhilHealth" account, which has a balance of ₱357,230.43. Additionally, the unsettled balance per the Statement of Premium Account (SPA) from PhilHealth, totaling ₱326,927.88, should be examined. Any confirmed outstanding amounts must be remitted to PhilHealth without delay.
We also recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer promptly coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to update employees' employment status and basic monthly salary. This will ensure that PhilHealth records reflect accurate and up-to-date information.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer ensure the full and timely remittance of both employee contributions and the employer’s share in compliance with PhilHealth's deadlines. Additionally, any interest charges incurred due to delayed payments in December 2023, January to March, and June 2024 should be settled immediately using personal funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Immediately compute the correct tax liability for each affected employee and official, encompassing the previously excluded Productivity Enhancement Incentive (PEI), Collective Negotiation Agreement (CNA), and the excess clothing allowance. The corresponding under withheld amount should be reflected in a payroll adjustment and remitted to the BIR as soon as possible, along with any applicable penalties or interest; and
ensure accurate differentiation between taxable and non-taxable income in accordance with BIR regulations and regularly review payroll tax computation to avoid penalties and interest due to under withholding.
We also recommended that the management, thru the Municipal Accountant, allow regular training for relevant staff on current tax laws and BIR pronouncements to ensure ongoing compliance and prevent future under withholding.

We recommended that the Local School Board and the Municipal Accountant diligently comply with the reportorial requirements in the allocation and utilization on Special Education Fund as outlined in the afore-cited regulations.

We recommended (1) that (1) the LGSO together with the Inventory Committee conduct physical count of all LGU properties as soon as possible, (2) mark the counted assets with property tags and (3) prepare and submit the Reports on Physical Count of PPE to the Municipal Accountant, together with the copy thereof on or before March 31, 2024.

We recommended (4) that the Municipal Accountant: (4) obtain the monthly bank statements from the depository bank promptly, (5) prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation regularly, and (6) submit the same to the Audit Team within 10 days after the end of each month; and (7) prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to record the valid book reconciling items per Bank Reconciliation Statements on the month that they occurred or were detected, in order to present an accurate and reliable cash balance in the financial statements.

We recommended (8) that the Municipal Accountant (8)reclassify the CIP - Infrastructure Assets account amounting ₱3,562,855.51 to Road Networks account, (9) revert the amount of ₱1,729,800.31 to the Due to NGAs account and (10) record the same as addition to Road Networks account.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert the amount of ₱2,364,199.50 to the Due to NGAs account and record the same as deduction from the Trust Liabilities - LDRRMF account.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the issuance of inventories for distribution as expense amounting ₱754,800.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the uncollected water bills and stall rentals as of December 31, 2023. We also reiterated our recommendation in CY 2021 AAR that the Head of the Billing Section submit to the Municipal Accountant the monthly billing report of the LGU’s water system, and the Acting Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the monthly Billing Statements of stall rentals so that the pertinent Receivables and Income will be recorded in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments for the above erroneous entry and record the procurement of relief goods for distribution and stockpiling using the Welfare Goods for Distribution account.

We commended the efforts made by Management in the efficient and effective accomplishment of the identified priority development projects in CY 2023, especially that all expenditures charged to the 20% Development Fund were not among the itemized disbursements prohibited in Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1. (17) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned fast track the implementation of the remaining unimplemented development projects and continuously adhere to the provisions of the aforementioned DBM-DOF-DILG JMC, to ensure that maximum attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality are achieved and the intended purposes of the 20% Development Fund are realized.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (18) issue Demand Letters to Barangays with outstanding unliquidated Fund Transfers; (19) once liquidated and for purposes of future fund transfers, prepare and attach certification that funds previously transferred to the Implementing Agency (IA) have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before signifying in the Disbursement Voucher the propriety and completeness of supporting documents.; (20) make the necessary entries for the unrecorded fund transfer to Brgy. Esperanza; and, (21) determine the accountable barangays for the previous year’s unliquidated balances amounting to ₱3,967,623.28 and demand the liquidation of the same. (22) Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stop issuing checks for Financial Assistance to Barangays with unliquidated Fund Transfers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the MDRRM Council, and the MDRRM Officer: (23) Implement during the year the remaining 3 PPAs identified in the LDRRMFIP in order to effectively increase the level of preparedness of the municipality during natural calamities; (24) Provide specific timelines and implement the PPAs under the LDRRMFIP within the said timelines; (25) We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer, MDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant ensure that the LDRRMF are appropriated and spent solely for disaster-related activities as contemplated in the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG’s JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit justification for the lacking documents which are indispensable prior to allocating funds for confidential expenses. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned comply with the COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 for proper allocation of confidential fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE): (28) Limit the hiring of Job Order workers to indispensable functions only and pay the prescribed daily minimum wage as prescribed by the Regional Tripartite Wages and Productivity Boards for Region VIII; (29) Prioritize the hiring for vacant Plantilla Positions, subject to the prescribed PS limitation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the LGSO locate the variances noted. (31) Adjustments/corrections shall be made in the books and/or in the property records to put the accounts in order.

We recommended that the Supply Officer report all unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties and expedite the disposal of the said properties through appropriate mode of disposal, as determined by the Disposal Committee and update the Inventory Custodian Slips of those properties that are still functional. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant drop the aforementioned properties in the PPE account to arrive at a correct account balance.

We recommended that the Supply Officer report all unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties and expedite the disposal of the said properties through appropriate mode of disposal, as determined by the Disposal Committee. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant derecognize the unserviceable properties as soon as the disposal is done.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in the preparation of succeeding Financial Statements, shall provide detailed information as to the composition of each loan account in the Notes to the Financial Statement. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (40) record the unrecorded November 2022 loan payment; (41) trace the root cause of the unreconciled variance in the Loans Payable – Domestic account balance and (42) make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the same and to reflect the correct balance of the same.

We recommended that the Sanggunian and its Presiding Officer submit written explanation/justification on the approval of reversions despite their non-compliance with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations on reversions.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer record all the donations-in-kind received by the RHU in the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, duly supported with the Acknowledgment Receipts, proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries not necessarily in the required form, and the inventory of the remaining undistributed items, if any; and submit a copy of the same to the Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant. We also recommended that the Supply Officer, assist the Municipal Health Officer in the inspection and acceptance of all donations-in-kind, and in the conduct of inventory of the remaining undistributed items, if any. We further recommended that the Supply Officer maintain copies of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and its supporting documents and prepare and maintain a Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM. Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, based on the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM, make a disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statement as to quantity received, issued and balance of all donation-in-kind received by the Municipality, with categorization as to food items, medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits and others.

We recommended that the General Service Officer immediately issue Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) to the person-in charge/ user of the received donated rescue vehicle. (53) the General Services Officer to transfer the ownership per LTO Records from Provincial Government to the Municipal Government of Leyte.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create the Municipal Management Team tasked to monitor and prepare quarterly report to follow-up on the implementation and status of the project. We also recommended that the Municipal Management Team to conduct inventory of remaining alive seedlings, evaluate the current condition of the seedlings and conduct training on beneficiaries with surviving seedling on how to properly care and cultivate the seedlings. Moving forward, we recommended that Municipal Mayor must ensure that all livelihood projects are properly implemented so as not to waste government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (56) issue a directive to the Department Heads of the Municipality to prepare a quarterly report on Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances on the donations that will be received by their offices in the period covering the State of Calamity, including those received by the Mayor’s Office, and submit the said report to the General Services Officer. We also recommended that the General Services Officer prepare the Consolidated Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances based on the reports submitted by the Heads of Department, and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the same report if cash donations are received. The said report should be submitted to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, and copies should be furnished to the Municipal Accountant and the Audit Team.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the submission of monitoring report from the Management Team and furnish a copy to the Auditor for validation.

We recommended that: The Municipal Mayor direct each LGU Officials responsible in the accounting and reporting of the local road network of the LGU to (61) follow strictly the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008 and (62) submit the correct reports at year-end. Also, we recommended that: The Municipal Assessor determine the cost of the road lot component for each road project of the LGU for recognition in the financial statements; The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components of road projects. Also, submit the Inventory of Local Roads to DILG immediately and every three years thereafter, without need for demand from the said Office. The General Services Office prepare and maintain complete Property Cards with the necessary data and reconcile their records with the Accounting Office.
We recommended to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval of any proposed revisions on the Tax Ordinance;
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer institute legal remedies provided for in Section 256 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the recorded valuation of the land account be traced to its acquisition cost or appraised value. Subsequently, property cards should be maintained for these properties and ownership of the lots should be established as basis for recording the parcels of land in the books of accounts. It is also suggested that the Local Chief Executive should clearly identify, thru a memorandum, the responsible office / officer who will take custody of the land documents and the titling of these properties. The certificates of title should be registered in the name of the municipality with the Register of Deeds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit a written justification
for the Audit Team’s evaluation for awarding the pakyaw contract amounting
to ₱900,000.00 to only one pakyaw group which is more than what is allowed
in Appendix 1 of the IRR of RA 9184 and why workers who are not members
of the winning pakyaw group were allowed to work and were paid with the
total amount of ₱425,000.00 despite absence of contract.

For similar project in the future, we recommended that the BAC strictly
comply with the provision of Section 4.8 of the Revised Guidelines for the
implementation of Infrastructure Projects by Administration to provide work
to as many laborers in the barangay as possible by grouping pakyaw workers
based on the lump sum works of the project. This recommendation is without

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the Municipal Mayor to require the designated General
Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their respective records
pertaining to the properties of the LGU. Also, the designated GSO maintain the
complete and updated property records, specifically the Property Cards.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require each program and project
coordinator to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects funded by the
National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA and
require them to submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents to the Source
Agency immediately.

We recommend that the Municipal DRRMO prepare annually the
LDRRMFIP in the prescribed format to support the amounts and the activities
indicated in the Annual Investment Plan of the LGU with details of the
programs/projects/activities to be funded from the current year 70% Mitigation
fund including those to be funded from the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of
previous years. Also, utilize fully the 70% Mitigation Fund for projects which will
enhance the capability of the LGU to cope with disasters.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning Development Officer (MPDO)
include only in the LGUs Annual Investment Plan priority projects which are
capital in nature and those authorized to be funded from the 20% Development
Fund. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor approve the utilization of
the 20% development fund only for disbursements which are related to and/ or in
connection with the implementation of development projects in order to attain
environmental and socio-economic development in the Municipality of Inopacan.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the requesting barangays
to sign a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the municipality and submit
Budget Proposals/Program of Work, approved SB Resolutions and other necessary
documents before funds are transferred to the recipient barangays. It should be
stipulated in the MOA that liquidation of Financial Assistance (FA) should be made
immediately after the project has been implemented.

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all recipient
barangays to submit Fund Utilization Report (FUR) together with the necessary
attachments, as soon as the intended program/project is implemented and defer the
grant of additional financial assistance unless the previous FA have been
liquidated.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board of Inopacan, Leyte,
through the Mayor as Chairman, maximize the use of the SEF for the improvement
of educational facilities and other priority programs/projects/activities enumerated
in DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 1998 to ensure that the desired benefits
are availed by the students. Also, specify in the budget for SEF the school in which
the funds are to be utilized.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board to construct the central Materials Recovery Facility
(MRF) in a way that it can accommodate efficient and safe materials processing,
movement, and storage. Further, we also suggest considering the fencing of the
dumpsite to prevent and control possible unauthorized access and littering in the
area.

Also, provide or allocate budget to purchase 1 unit dump truck to cover the
collection of solid waste and support the related activities of Solid Waste

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should be strict in the review of the transactions to ensure completeness and validity of supporting documents in conformity with the COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend that management, though its Focal Point Chairperson prepares and submits its GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval in conformity with the relevant provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No 2013-1 to ensure that all GAD related expenditures are aligned to the GAD Plan and Budget.
We recommend that the Municipal Government to strictly deduct the withholding taxes and remit immediately the taxes withheld to BIR Office on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Government submit sufficient legal basis in the granting of additional Christmas Bonus to all employees.

We recommend prudence in the utilization and use of government funds be exercised and observed at all times by management to prevent incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, extravagant and unconscionable expenditures.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant must observe strictly COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2009-002 in the granting of cash advances. Elective Officials should not be granted cash advances other than travel, except for Intelligence Fund which should be liquidated immediately. We further recommend the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advance.

We recommend that the management ensures the existence of a valid Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement and resolution from the Sanggunian Bayan prior to the transfer of funds from various government agencies, especially if disbursements were charged therefrom, in accordance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2012-001.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a JEV and make necessary adjustment of the account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the management utilizes its 20% economic development fund only for disbursements which are related to and/ or in connection with the implementation of development project, programs and activities.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should create a team to conduct the municipality’s physical inventory of the property, plant and equipment at least once a year and prepare an inventory report for reconciliation purposes. Likewise, the municipal treasurer should maintain property cards per category of property, plant and equipment in order to determine the unserviceable and non-existent properties for eventual dropping of these in the PPE accounts pursuant to Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386, Sections 119 and 124 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

In all instances, the “Other MOE” account shall be appropriately used solely to record transactions for which they are prescribed under the COA rules and regulations.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the implemention thru adherence to the prescribed process initially by the provision of budgetary allocations at the prescribed amount.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Treasurer to prepare separate reports for each economic enterprise and (b) Municipal Accountant to maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for each type of economic enterprise.
